content.problemStatement:
Being a newcomer to Computopia, Chef is confused by the countless subway lines in the city. Help him!

You are given an undirected graph with NNN vertices and MMM edges. There are no self-loops and no simple cycles that contain more than 2 edges in the graph (i.e. if we replaced each set of multiple edges connecting the same pair of vertices by one edge, we would obtain a tree). Each edge also has a colour.

Consider a simple path with length KKK in the graph that passes through edges with colours A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AKA_1, A_2, \dots, A_KA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AK‚Äã in this order. The cost of this path is defined as (A1‚â†A2)+(A2‚â†A3)+‚ãØ+(AK‚àí1‚â†AK)(A_1 \neq A_2) + (A_2 \neq A_3) + \dots + (A_{K-1} \neq A_K)(A1‚ÄãÓÄ†=A2‚Äã)+(A2‚ÄãÓÄ†=A3‚Äã)+‚ãØ+(AK‚àí1‚ÄãÓÄ†=AK‚Äã), where the boolean value `true` is interpreted as the integer 111 and `false` is interpreted as 000. In other words, the cost of a simple path is the number of pairs of neighbouring (consecutive) edges on this path with different colours. The cost of a path with length 000 is defined to be 000.

You need to answer QQQ queries. In each query, you are given vertices uuu and vvv; you should find the maximum possible cost of some simple path connecting them.

Note that a simple path may not visit a vertex more than once.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
Each of the next MMM lines contains three space-separated integers uuu, vvv and www denoting an edge between vertices uuu and vvv with colour www.
The next line contains an integer QQQ.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the maximum cost of a simple path.
